sql: WITH StoreRankingByDistrict AS (SELECT s.Store_Name, d.District_name, s.Ranking, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY d.District_name ORDER BY s.Ranking ASC, s.Area_size DESC) as district_rank FROM store s JOIN store_district sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID JOIN district d ON sd.District_ID = d.District_ID) SELECT Store_Name, District_name, district_rank FROM StoreRankingByDistrict;
result_summary: This query ranks stores within each district based on their original ranking and area size, returning the store name, district name, and the calculated rank.

SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1

  SELECT T2.dept_name

sql:
SELECT
  T1.department_name,
  COUNT(T2.staff_id)
FROM Departments AS T1
JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2
  ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id
WHERE
  T2.job_title_code = 'Sales Person'
GROUP BY
  T1.department_name;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, COUNT(T3.product_id) AS number_of_products, AVG(T3.product_price) AS average_product_price
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
JOIN Products AS T3
  ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id
GROUP BY
  T1.supplier_name;

sql:
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Completed'
INTERSECT
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'New'

sql: WITH StudentAttendanceCount AS (SELECT student_id, COUNT(course_id) AS attendance_count FROM Student_Course_Attendance GROUP BY student_id) SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, COALESCE(sac.attendance_count, 0) AS total_attendance FROM People AS p JOIN People_Addresses AS pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses AS a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id LEFT JOIN StudentAttendanceCount AS sac ON p.person_id = sac.student_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-03-01' AND p.person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students);

sql: WITH StatisticsStudents AS (SELECT scr.student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations scr WHERE scr.course_id = '301'), PassedCandidates AS (SELECT ca.candidate_id FROM Candidate_Assessments ca WHERE ca.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass') SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, a.state_province_county FROM People p JOIN StatisticsStudents ss ON p.person_id = ss.student_id JOIN PassedCandidates pc ON p.person_id = pc.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-01-01';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT C.customer_name, C.customer_email FROM Customers AS C JOIN Customer_Orders AS CO ON C.customer_id = CO.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS OI ON CO.order_id = OI.order_id JOIN Products AS P ON OI.product_id = P.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS PS ON P.product_id = PS.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS S ON PS.supplier_id = S.supplier_id WHERE S.supplier_name = 'Tesco' AND CO.order_status_code = 'Completed';

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD)
FROM region AS T1
JOIN affected_region AS T2
  ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id
JOIN storm AS T3
  ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Region_name;

sql: SELECT SUM(T4.chargeable_amount) AS total_chargeable_value FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Assets AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.supplier_company_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T4 ON T3.part_id = T4.part_id WHERE T1.company_name = 'McDermott Group' AND T4.chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the total chargeable value of all parts supplied by the 'McDermott Group' that are marked as chargeable.

result_summary: Fetches the name and total cost for the single dog breed with the highest cumulative cost of treatments.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN T3.Market_Value_in_Billion > 400 THEN 'High Value' WHEN T3.Market_Value_in_Billion > 200 THEN 'Mid Value' ELSE 'Low Value' END AS Market_Category, COUNT(T1.People_ID) AS Employee_Count FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID GROUP BY Market_Category;
result_summary: Retrieve the total count of employees grouped by their company's market value category, which is defined as 'High', 'Mid', or 'Low Value'.

sql: SELECT P_org.Party_Details AS Organizer, E.Event_ID, P_part.Party_Details AS Participant FROM Events AS E JOIN Parties_in_Events AS PIE_org ON E.Event_ID = PIE_org.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS P_org ON PIE_org.Party_ID = P_org.Party_ID JOIN Parties_in_Events AS PIE_part ON E.Event_ID = PIE_part.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS P_part ON PIE_part.Party_ID = P_part.Party_ID WHERE P_org.Party_Details = 'European Free Alliance' AND PIE_org.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND PIE_part.Role_Code = 'Participant' AND P_org.Party_ID <> P_part.Party_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the event ID and the names of the participants for all events organized by the 'European Free Alliance'.

Database: solvency_ii

sql: WITH SalesAgentSales AS (SELECT E.EmployeeId, E.FirstName, E.LastName, I.Total FROM Employee AS E JOIN Customer AS C ON E.EmployeeId = C.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice AS I ON C.CustomerId = I.CustomerId WHERE E.Title = 'Sales Support Agent') SELECT FirstName, LastName, COUNT(Total) AS TotalInvoices, SUM(Total) AS TotalSalesValue FROM SalesAgentSales GROUP BY EmployeeId, FirstName, LastName ORDER BY TotalSalesValue DESC;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, total number of invoices, and total sales value for each sales support agent, ordered by the highest total sales value.

sql: SELECT A.Name AS ArtistName, COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) AS NumberOfGenres FROM Artist A JOIN Album AL ON A.ArtistId = AL.ArtistId JOIN Track T ON AL.AlbumId = T.AlbumId JOIN Genre G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId GROUP BY A.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) > 1 ORDER BY NumberOfGenres DESC;
result_summary: Retrieve the names of artists with more than one genre and the count of their genres, ordered by the genre count in descending order.

sql: WITH CustomerSpending AS (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) SELECT C.FirstName, C.LastName, CS.TotalSpent FROM Customer C JOIN CustomerSpending CS ON C.CustomerId = CS.CustomerId WHERE CS.TotalSpent > (SELECT AVG(TotalSpent) FROM CustomerSpending) AND C.Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total spending for customers from the USA whose spending is greater than the average spending of all customers.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.year FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.age = (SELECT MIN(age) FROM Person);

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1
WHERE T1.dept_name IN (
  SELECT T2.dept_name
  FROM course AS T2
  WHERE T2.credits > 3
  GROUP BY T2.dept_name
  HAVING count(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Fetch the average instructor salary for each department that offers more than one course with a credit value greater than 3.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary: Fetch the total value of products purchased from each supplier, limited to products that have been part of completed customer orders.

sql: WITH PartyWins AS (SELECT p.Party, SUM(CASE WHEN dp.Affirmative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' THEN 1 WHEN dp.Negative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Wins FROM people p JOIN debate_people dp ON p.People_ID = dp.Affirmative OR p.People_ID = dp.Negative GROUP BY p.Party) SELECT Party, Wins FROM PartyWins WHERE Wins = (SELECT MAX(Wins) FROM PartyWins);

result_summary: This query retrieves the name and points of climbers who have climbed 'Mount Kenya (Batian)' and whose points are greater than the average points of all climbers.

Table: mountain

- (3, 'Mawenzi (Hans Meyer Pk)', 5148.0, 850.0, 'Kilimanjaro', 'Tanzania')

Table: climber
Columns: Climber_ID, Name, Country, Time, Points, Mountain_ID

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year JOIN instructor AS T4 ON T3.ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.salary > (SELECT AVG(T5.salary) FROM instructor AS T5 WHERE T5.dept_name = T4.dept_name) GROUP BY T1.ID, T1.name, T1.dept_name

sql:
SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Race_ID)
FROM track AS T1
JOIN race AS T2
  ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Track_ID
HAVING
  COUNT(T2.Race_ID) > (
    SELECT
      AVG(Race_Count)
    FROM (
      SELECT
        COUNT(Race_ID) AS Race_Count
      FROM race
      GROUP BY
        Track_ID
    ) AS T3
  );
result_summary: Return the names of tracks and the number of races they've hosted, but only for those tracks with a race count higher than the overall average number of races per track.

sql:
SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname
FROM (
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1
  INTERSECT
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 2
) AS T1
JOIN Authors AS T2
  ON T1.authID = T2.authID;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors who have been listed as both the first and the second author on a publication.

sql:
SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total = 315.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Clean_Jerk = 175.0

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID  =  T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating  >  (SELECT avg(T3.Seating) FROM track AS T3 JOIN race AS T4 ON T3.Track_ID  =  T4.Track_ID)

sql: WITH CompanyEngineers AS (SELECT T1.engineer_id FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'Langworth-Funk') SELECT T5.first_name, T5.last_name, T1.asset_details, T3.visit_start_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN CompanyEngineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T5 ON T4.engineer_id = T5.engineer_id;

sql: WITH IndustryAssets AS (SELECT Industry, SUM(Assets_in_Billion) AS TotalAssets FROM company GROUP BY Industry), IndustryTenure AS (SELECT c.Industry, AVG(e.Year_working) AS AvgTenure FROM company c JOIN employment e ON c.Company_ID = e.Company_ID GROUP BY c.Industry) SELECT ia.Industry, ia.TotalAssets, it.AvgTenure FROM IndustryAssets ia JOIN IndustryTenure it ON ia.Industry = it.Industry WHERE ia.TotalAssets > 700 AND it.AvgTenure > 1;

sql: SELECT P.Name AS PlaylistName, MT.Name AS MediaTypeName, COUNT(T.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Playlist P JOIN PlaylistTrack PT ON P.PlaylistId = PT.PlaylistId JOIN Track T ON PT.TrackId = T.TrackId JOIN MediaType MT ON T.MediaTypeId = MT.MediaTypeId GROUP BY P.Name, MT.Name ORDER BY P.Name, TrackCount DESC;
result_summary:

sql: WITH MonthlyTemps AS (SELECT City_ID, 'Jan' as Month, Jan as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Feb' as Month, Feb as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Jul' as Month, Jul as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Dec' as Month, Dec as Temp FROM temperature) SELECT T2.City, T1.Month, T1.Temp FROM MonthlyTemps AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Temp = (SELECT MAX(Temp) FROM MonthlyTemps WHERE City_ID = T1.City_ID) AND T1.City_ID = 2;

sql: WITH StudentRegistrationCount AS (SELECT student_id, COUNT(course_id) as num_courses FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY student_id) SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, src.num_courses FROM StudentRegistrationCount src JOIN People p ON src.student_id = p.person_id WHERE src.num_courses > (SELECT AVG(num_courses) FROM StudentRegistrationCount);

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM operate_company AS T3 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T3.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T1 ON T2.airport_id = T1.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T4.airport_id FROM flight AS T4 GROUP BY T4.airport_id HAVING avg(T4.Altitude) > 3000) EXCEPT SELECT T5.name FROM operate_company AS T5 WHERE T5.Incorporated_in = 'China'

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, sum(T2.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id  =  T2.supplier_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id  =  T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_type_code  =  'Clothes' GROUP BY T1.supplier_name HAVING sum(T2.total_value_purchased)  >  (SELECT sum(T5.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T4 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id  =  T5.supplier_id WHERE T4.supplier_name  =  'Tesco')
result_summary: Fetch the supplier name and total purchased value for suppliers of 'Clothes' whose total value is greater than the total purchased value from the supplier 'Tesco'.

sql: WITH AverageLifts AS (SELECT AVG(Snatch) as AvgSnatch, AVG(Clean_Jerk) as AvgCleanJerk FROM body_builder) SELECT p.Name FROM people p JOIN body_builder b ON p.People_ID = b.People_ID, AverageLifts al WHERE b.Snatch > al.AvgSnatch AND b.Clean_Jerk > al.AvgCleanJerk;

sql: WITH CollegeParticipation AS (SELECT c.College_ID, c.Name AS CollegeName, COUNT(DISTINCT r.Decoration_Theme) AS UniqueThemes FROM college c JOIN member m ON c.College_ID = m.College_ID JOIN round r ON m.Member_ID = r.Member_ID GROUP BY c.College_ID, c.Name) SELECT CollegeName, UniqueThemes FROM CollegeParticipation WHERE UniqueThemes = (SELECT MAX(UniqueThemes) FROM CollegeParticipation);

sql: SELECT sa.AirportName, COUNT(f.FlightNo) AS NumberOfDepartingFlights FROM flights AS f JOIN airports AS sa ON TRIM(f.SourceAirport) = sa.AirportCode WHERE TRIM(sa.Country) = 'United States' GROUP BY sa.AirportName;

sql: SELECT T.TrackId, T.Name AS TrackName, G.Name AS GenreName FROM Track AS T JOIN Genre AS G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId WHERE T.TrackId IN (SELECT PT.TrackId FROM PlaylistTrack AS PT JOIN Playlist AS P ON PT.PlaylistId = P.PlaylistId WHERE P.Name = 'Music') AND T.TrackId NOT IN (SELECT T2.TrackId FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Genre AS G2 ON T2.GenreId = G2.GenreId WHERE G2.Name = 'Rock');
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and genre for all tracks that are part of the 'Music' playlist, excluding those of the 'Rock' genre.

sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, i.InvoiceId, i.Total, (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM Invoice WHERE CustomerId = c.CustomerId) AS CustomerAverageInvoice FROM Customer AS c JOIN Invoice AS i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.Country = 'Germany' ORDER BY c.CustomerId, i.InvoiceDate;
result_summary: This query fetches the first name, last name, invoice ID, and total for each invoice, alongside the customer's overall average invoice total, specifically for customers in Germany, ordered by customer and then by invoice date.

sql: SELECT C.Country, G.Name as TopGenre, MAX(T.PurchaseCount) AS Purchases FROM (SELECT IL.TrackId, COUNT(*) AS PurchaseCount FROM InvoiceLine IL GROUP BY IL.TrackId) T JOIN Track TR ON T.TrackId = TR.TrackId JOIN Genre G ON TR.GenreId = G.GenreId JOIN Invoice I ON T.TrackId = (SELECT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine WHERE InvoiceId = I.InvoiceId LIMIT 1) JOIN Customer C ON I.CustomerId = C.CustomerId GROUP BY C.Country, G.Name ORDER BY C.Country, Purchases DESC;
result_summary: Fetches a list of music genres for each country, ranked by the sales count of the most-purchased track within that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, AVG(T2.Weeks_on_Top) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING AVG(T2.Weeks_on_Top) > 1.5;
result_summary: This query fetches the name and average weeks on top for each artist who has an average of more than 1.5 weeks on top.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;

nl_question: What is the total value of products purchased from each supplier, specifically for items that have been part of completed customer orders?

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Calculate the total purchase value from each supplier, considering only products that were part of completed customer orders.
